rawContent: {"stage":"review-fix","insights":[{"kind":"gotcha","text":"Review-fix runs that touch test files can introduce new TypeScript compilation errors even while fixing runtime issues — always run build after fixing tests.","scope":"src/routes/notifications.test.ts"},{"kind":"lesson","text":"Tests for singleton-based services (e.g. default store) that mutate global state must run last or be isolated with beforeEach/teardown to avoid polluting subsequent tests in the same file.","scope":"src/services/notificationService.test.ts"},{"kind":"gotcha","text":"Patching a stub back to a real implementation at the end of a test without proper teardown causes singleton pollution that silently breaks unrelated tests in the same suite.","scope":"src/services/notificationService.test.ts"},{"kind":"decision","text":"Request-time service instantiation (vs. module-level) was the correct fix for the original bug — it allows request-scoped overrides and prevents shared mutable state across concurrent requests
